Package tellui.core.effect

Class Transition

java.lang.Object
tellui.core.effect.Transition
public final class Transition extends Object
Represents a transition animation between two UI containers.

A transition defines the start and end spatial states (position and size) for both the previous (outgoing) and current (incoming) containers. The library provides four predefined slide transitions: SLIDE_LEFT, SLIDE_RIGHT, SLIDE_UP, SLIDE_DOWN. Custom transitions can be created by instantiating this class and setting the four spatial states appropriately.

See Also:
  • SpatialState

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final Transition
    SLIDE_DOWN
    Slide transition where the new content enters from the bottom, pushing the old content out to the top.
    static final Transition
    SLIDE_LEFT
    Slide transition where the new content enters from the left, pushing the old content out to the right.
    static final Transition
    SLIDE_RIGHT
    Slide transition where the new content enters from the right, pushing the old content out to the left.
    static final Transition
    SLIDE_UP
    Slide transition where the new content enters from the top, pushing the old content out to the bottom.

  • Field Details

    • SLIDE_LEFT

      public static final Transition SLIDE_LEFT
      Slide transition where the new content enters from the left, pushing the old content out to the right.

    • SLIDE_RIGHT

      public static final Transition SLIDE_RIGHT
      Slide transition where the new content enters from the right, pushing the old content out to the left.

    • SLIDE_UP

      public static final Transition SLIDE_UP
      Slide transition where the new content enters from the top, pushing the old content out to the bottom.

    • SLIDE_DOWN

      public static final Transition SLIDE_DOWN
      Slide transition where the new content enters from the bottom, pushing the old content out to the top.

  • Method Details

    • getCurrentStart

      public final SpatialState getCurrentStart()
      Returns the starting spatial state of the incoming (current) container.
      Returns:
      the current start state (never null)

    • setCurrentStart

      public final void setCurrentStart(SpatialState currentStart)
      Sets the starting spatial state of the incoming (current) container.
      Parameters:
      currentStart - the start state (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if currentStart is null

    • getCurrentEnd

      public final SpatialState getCurrentEnd()
      Returns the ending spatial state of the incoming (current) container.
      Returns:
      the current end state (never null)

    • setCurrentEnd

      public final void setCurrentEnd(SpatialState currentEnd)
      Sets the ending spatial state of the incoming (current) container.
      Parameters:
      currentEnd - the end state (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if currentEnd is null

    • getPreviousStart

      public final SpatialState getPreviousStart()
      Returns the starting spatial state of the outgoing (previous) container.
      Returns:
      the previous start state (never null)

    • setPreviousStart

      public final void setPreviousStart(SpatialState previousStart)
      Sets the starting spatial state of the outgoing (previous) container.
      Parameters:
      previousStart - the start state (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if previousStart is null

    • getPreviousEnd

      public final SpatialState getPreviousEnd()
      Returns the ending spatial state of the outgoing (previous) container.
      Returns:
      the previous end state (never null)

    • setPreviousEnd

      public final void setPreviousEnd(SpatialState previousEnd)
      Sets the ending spatial state of the outgoing (previous) container.
      Parameters:
      previousEnd - the end state (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if previousEnd is null
